description_long: This is a two-story house with a steep gable roof covered in gray shingles and accented by brown ridge tiles. The exterior walls are clad in gray stone or brick, with prominent vertical and horizontal wooden beams creating a half-timbered appearance. The front facade features two large, square windows on the upper floor and a central wooden door below, flanked by additional windows. The side and rear elevations have fewer windows, with the rear wall lacking any visible openings. The house sits on a patch of textured ground, suggesting a yard or garden area. The overall design is simple yet sturdy, evoking traditional European residential architecture.
description: A two-story, gray stone house with a steep gable roof, half-timbered accents, and large square windows on the front facade.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entry door, two large upper windows, and symmetrical half-timbered detailing.
description_view_1: Side view with a single window on the upper floor and visible roof slope, highlighting the house's depth.
description_view_2: Rear view with no windows or doors, displaying the full gable and timber framing.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with no windows, emphasizing the solid wall and timber grid pattern.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical rows on front, minimal on sides and rear
attribute_entryway_design: Central wooden door with no porch or steps, framed by timber accents and flanked by large windows
